findings.summary: GET /v1/connectors/drive/connection/{id}/skipped returns 200 with empty groups for a non-existent connection id, while the parent GET /v1/connectors/drive/connection/{id} returns 404 (CONNECTION_NOT_FOUND). Inconsistent error contract; no data exposure.

GET /v1/connectors/drive/connection/{id} with non-UUID path param returns 500 with raw 'invalid input syntax for type uuid: "not-a-uuid" (SQLSTATE 22P02)' message. No injection (parameterized query rejected the cast); validate UUID at handler boundary and return 400 with stable error code.

POST /api/search with control-character query (\u0000\u0001 + injection-style suffix) returns 500 SEARCH_FAILED. Other malformed queries return clean 200 text_fallback. Strip/reject control characters at handler.

OAuth callback redirect URL reflects raw provider/database error string ('error=google%3A+lookup+oauth+state%3A+no+rows+in+result+set') to the browser. Emit stable error code (e.g. error=oauth_state_invalid) instead of leaking internal wording.

Formal feature-wide security audit for spec 038 at HEAD e456c09 (66 commits ahead of origin/main). Verdict: ⚠️ FINDINGS — proceed to bubbles.validate. Phase 1 threat model (10 surface entries) recorded in report.md#phase-1-threat-model. Phase 2 dependency vulnerability scan via PATH=$HOME/go/bin:$PATH govulncheck@v1.3.0 (DB updated 2026-04-21) on ./internal/drive/... ./internal/api/... EXIT=0 surfaced 22 reachable Go-stdlib vulnerabilities at go1.24.3 (crypto/x509, crypto/tls, encoding/pem, html/template, net/http, syscall, etc. — fixed in go1.24.8 most, go1.25.9 for 3); plus 8 third-party + 7 transitive vulnerabilities NOT reachable from running 038 code paths. Phase 3 OWASP code-review grep sweep on internal/drive/, internal/api/drive_*.go, internal/telegram/drive*.go: zero hardcoded secrets, zero secrets-in-logs (slog.* calls in save/retrieve/extract log only provider/connection_id/rule_id/target_path/size_bytes/error string — never a token, file byte, or extracted-text payload per design §12), zero TODO/FIXME/HACK markers, zero math/rand uses (state token correctly via crypto/rand at internal/drive/google/google.go:374-380), zero InsecureSkipVerify, zero exec.Command/os.Exec shell-exec calls. Two fmt.Sprintf-in-SQL sites surfaced and verified safe (CLOSED-AS-SAFE): internal/drive/scan/service.go:399 column hardcoded enum 'tombstoned_at' or 'permission_lost_at'; internal/api/drive_save_handlers.go:241 fixed-shape WHERE clauses with $N placeholders, all user values bound via args. One IDOR-pattern site (internal/api/drive_handlers.go:158,173) — explicitly documented design Scope 1 trust deferral pending platform-auth integration (S-003 LOW, ROUTED to bubbles.harden as MIT-038-S-003). 12 unbounded io.ReadAll sites enumerated; bytes path in internal/drive/extract/service.go:255 correctly enforces io.LimitReader(_, maxFileSizeBytes+1); 10 google.go HTTP response sites flagged S-004 LOW (provider URLs are SST-trusted; ROUTED as MIT-038-S-004 defense-in-depth backlog). Phase 4 A-001 reconciliation — formally CLOSED-AS-ACCEPTED MVP posture per design.md §2.3 + §11 decision-log A1; mitigation routed as MIT-038-S-001 (move OAuth access + refresh tokens out of drive_connections.credentials_ref plaintext into approved secret storage when a non-Google provider's token model first demands it). Four hardening options evaluated and rejected (option D selected — confirm reconciliation, no LOC change): refresh-token persistence + drive_credentials child table is out of scope per design §2.3; string→[]byte zero-after-use refactor across save/retrieve/scan/extract is non-cheap and defense-theater (Go strings immutable, http.Header retains string copies); crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare on 'bearer:' prefix would be cargo-cult crypto (format check, not auth boundary). Findings table: 0 CRITICAL · 0 HIGH · 2 MEDIUM (S-001 closed-as-accepted, S-002 routed-to-harden) · 2 LOW (S-003 closed-as-accepted, S-004 routed-to-harden) · 4 INFO (S-005/S-006 closed-as-safe false positives, S-007/S-008 clean). executionHistory.scope: Cross-cutting e2e infra (postgres cold-start readiness flake)
executionHistory.summary: Round 9 stabilize pass routed by bubbles.workflow to diagnose and fix the cross-cutting e2e infra flake that blocked Scope 1 DoD-8 (broader e2e regression) and DoD-12 (full pipeline) for feature 038, and equivalently blocked specs 037 and 039 broader e2e rollups. Verified root cause via controlled reproduction (docker compose down --volumes -> up -> tight curl/psql probe loop): three contributing surfaces, all reproducible. (1) ./smackerel.sh up was invoking 'docker compose up -d' WITHOUT --wait, so the CLI returned as soon as containers were created/started, not when their healthchecks went green. (2) The postgres healthcheck used unsuffixed 'pg_isready -U $USER -d $DB' which connects via the unix socket. During initdb's bootstrap, postgres briefly exposes a temp server on the unix socket, then shuts it down to start the real TCP server. pg_isready could succeed against the temp server, container marked healthy, compose unblocks dependents via service_healthy, the test runs 'docker compose exec postgres psql ...', and hits postgres mid-shutdown emitting 'FATAL: the database system is shutting down'. (3) tests/e2e/lib/helpers.sh::e2e_wait_healthy only required 'curl -sf $CORE_URL/api/health' to return any 2xx, but /api/health returns 200 with {"status":"degraded"} when core itself cannot reach postgres or has degraded subsystems (e.g. unwired connectors). Fix (minimum viable, three coordinated edits, no new defaults/fallbacks): (a) docker-compose.yml postgres healthcheck switched to 'pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB} -h localhost -p 5432 -q' (forces TCP, defeating the unix-socket initdb false positive) plus start_period: 15s and retries: 10. (b) smackerel.sh up command now passes '--wait --wait-timeout 180' to docker compose up -d. (c) tests/e2e/lib/helpers.sh::e2e_wait_healthy now requires HTTP 200 from /api/health AND a successful 'psql -tAc SELECT 1' round-trip against postgres before returning, and surfaces last http_code on timeout for diagnostics. Workflow mode bugfix-fastlane. VERDICT: completed_owned. APPROACH: wrap every io.ReadAll(resp.Body) site in internal/drive/google/google.go with io.LimitReader(_, cap) where cap flows from a new drive.io_limits.* SST surface. The wrap pattern mirrors the precedent set by MIT-040-S-006 commit abae5e6 (photos.io_limits.* + immich/photoprism upload caps + Telegram bot file caps): inline `var nameReader io.Reader = resp.Body; if cap > 0 { nameReader = io.LimitReader(resp.Body, cap) }; io.ReadAll(nameReader)` with the > 0 guard preserving legacy unbounded test wiring. SITE INVENTORY: audit cited 10 sites at lines 325/356/455/494/530/556/615/638/679 in google.go; actual count from `grep -nE 'io\.ReadAll' internal/drive/google/google.go` at HEAD is 9 (audit count off by one). All 9 wrapped. Other internal/drive/ sites left intentionally alone per audit: extract/service.go:255 already enforces maxFileSizeBytes+1 cap; extract/service.go:751 / memprovider/memprovider.go:264 / retrieve/postgres.go:163 do not consume HTTP response bodies. PER-SITE CAP MAPPING: oauth_response_max_bytes (64 KiB) → google.go:325 exchangeCodeForToken (token endpoint); provider_binary_max_bytes (500 MiB) → google.go:530 PutFile body.Reader (centralized in new readUploadBody helper for unit testability without database pool); provider_response_max_bytes (10 MiB) → 7 JSON metadata sites at google.go:356 (fetchAccountEmail), 455 (ListFolder), 494 (GetFile error), 556 (PutFile response), 615 (EnsureFolder GET error), 638 (EnsureFolder POST response), 679 (Changes).
